item.title: Backup snapshots directory created without mode 0700 — backup archives world-listable
item.intent: The backup snapshots directory MUST be created with mode 0700 so that backup archive existence and timestamps cannot be enumerated by other system users.
item.description: src/operate/backup/backup.ts line 166 calls mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option when creating ops/backup/snapshots/. This creates the snapshots directory with default permissions (0755), making it world-listable. Any user on the system can ls the snapshots directory to see that backups exist and read their timestamps and snapshot IDs. While the backup archives are GPG-encrypted, their existence and metadata should not be exposed. Fix: import DIR_MODE_SECRET from config/defaults.js (currently not imported — only BACKUP_GPG_TIMEOUT_MS is imported from defaults.js) and change mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). No chmodSync needed — snapshotsDir is always fresh-created at this call site.
steps: Read src/operate/backup/backup.ts lines 16-26 — confirm DIR_MODE_SECRET is not imported from defaults.js
Read src/operate/backup/backup.ts lines 155-170 — confirm mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Add DIR_MODE_SECRET to the existing import from ../../config/defaults.js (currently only BACKUP_GPG_TIMEOUT_MS is imported)
Change mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(snapsDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Check if src/operate/backup/backup.test.ts exists — if so, add a test that stats the snapshots directory and asserts mode & 0o777 === 0o700 after calling the backup function
Run npm test -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -20 to confirm all tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: The ops/backup/snapshots/ directory is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
DIR_MODE_SECRET is added to the import from config/defaults.js in backup.ts
All existing backup tests continue to pass
